| **Sen. Chuck Morse: Parents are being heard from Virginia to New Hampshire**
“I DON’T THINK parents should be telling schools what they should teach.”

Those words from former Virginia Governor Terry McAuliffe are why he will not be the future Governor Terry McAuliffe. During a debate with Republican challenger Glenn Youngkin in late September, McAuliffe defended his veto of a bill that would have let parents prevent their children from being exposed to materials they found objectionable.
The power of parents over their children’s education became the central issue of the gubernatorial campaign over the final month, with Youngkin stressing that parents should have ultimate control, while McAuliffe argued that parents should not be able to object to the decisions of public school officials.

On election night, Virginia parents settled the question, delivering a shocking upset win to Youngkin in a state that Joe Biden had carried by 10 points just one year ago. The power of parents to guide the education their children receive proved to be decisive in the most watched race in the nation.

Democrats attacked Youngkin for standing up for parents, falsely accusing him of wanting to ban books. They called him a racist for opposing the use of Critical Race Theory in public schools, arguing both that CRT didn’t really exist and that opposing it was a sign of White supremacy. Voters didn’t buy the smears.

Parents have the power to bring about political change. Politicians ignore them at their peril. In New Hampshire, Republicans at the State House have been listening to parents and empowering them to be more involved in their children’s education.

The COVID-19 pandemic put a huge strain on our students, and while some thrived, others struggled. We heard from parents that they needed more choices, and we listened. As part of the state budget, we created Education Freedom Accounts, which give parents the ability to direct how the state’s contribution to an adequate education is used. The program is limited to families earning 300% of the federal poverty level and structured similarly to New Hampshire’s successful Education Tax Credit Scholarship Program.

Throughout the budget debate, we were told that expanding school choice would dismantle public education. Nonsense. Most parents will always choose their local district school, but there is no one-size-fits-all approach to schools. Every child learns differently, and EFAs are a way to provide a great education for kids that aren’t thriving in a traditional classroom environment.

We provided new choices and parents responded. In the first year of this innovative program, 1,600 families in need have qualified for EFA scholarships. That’s 1,600 kids who will have a better path towards a great education.
Parents are concerned that radical left-wing theories such as Critical Race Theory have seeped into our schools. Young kids are taught that they are born privileged and racist. This is unacceptable and should never have been allowed to happen in our schools. So we included anti-discrimination language in the recent state budget, ensuring that schools will not teach that one race is inherently superior or inferior to another. We need to teach our children our history, including those times when America has struggled to live up to our founding ideals. But we must never teach them racist lessons.
Parents will be heard. If state legislators, school board members, or gubernatorial candidates ignore them, they will be heard at the voting booth. They will put themselves forward as candidates. They will give support to candidates who are willing to listen. They will rightly demand a greater say in what and how their children are taught.
We listened to parents in New Hampshire this year. Glenn Youngkin listened to them in Virginia. I expect their voices to be heard all across the country in 2022.

**Sen. Chuck Morse (R-Salem) is president of the New Hampshire Senate.**

clean_body: Stay up to date with the NHGOP! | | | | | | | | | | | | https:// ---|---|---|---|--- | Weekly Newsletter ** Note from Chairman Stepanek Weekly Governor's Glance **Upcoming Events House Republican Update Senate Republican Update Republican Spotlight Noteworthy News NHGOP Tweets NH Federation of Republican WomenNH Young RepublicansNH Federation of College Republicans | | Derek, Today will forever be remembered as the day Chris Pappas and Ann Kuster banded with Joe Biden to provide financial relief to the Democrat’s wealthiest at the expense of hard working families here in the Granite State. While blue collar workers in New Hampshire try to juggle sky-high inflation, peak pricing at the grocery stores and at the gas pump, our Federal Delegation’s only concern is an obscene giveaway for the richest residents in Democrat-led states across the country. This bill is not only incredibly irresponsible, it’s insulting to Americans across the country who are working hard to make ends meet. Too many Granite Staters aren't sure they’ll be able to put food on the table or afford fuel for their homes this winter. This bill puts already vulnerable Granite Staters in an even worse economic position, and Democrats couldn’t be bothered to care. Democrats here in New Hampshire, and down in Washington D.C., have repeatedly put their own self-interests ahead of their own constituents - and come next November, it won’t be forgotten. Congressman Chris Pappas and Congresswoman Ann Kuster will soon be left behind by voters as quickly as they abandoned Granite Staters over the last year. | Stephan Stepanek NHGOP Chairman | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | *NEW Check out our newest item on the NHGOP storefront - "Parents Not Politicians" bumper stickers! | | | Weekly G**overnor's Glance | | | "Follow Governor Chris Sununu on Twitter!" | | | November 22nd Canned Food Drive in Derry | | | "Get Info" | | December 3rd Christmas with Sen. Cotton | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 4th NHFCR State Address | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 5th Sounds of the Season | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 8th Rochester Christmas Party | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 9th Hampton Christmas Party | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 10th NHSRW Holiday Luncheon | | | "Get Tickets" | | December 15th Chairman's Eggnog Social | | | "Get Tickets" | | | | | Speaker Sherman Packard (R-Londonderry) and Majority Leader Jason Osborne (R-Auburn) released the following statements after the Education Committee passed HB255, as amended, on a bipartisan vote of 11-8. Speaker Packard "Today's vote relative to HB255 - coinciding with President Biden's visit - sent a message loud and clear: Granite Staters will not be ruled by an unconstitutional mandate. When it comes to standing up for what's right, NH has always led the way. We will continue to do that – together." "The Education Committee passed HB255, in the name of medical freedom. Employers are struggling to fill vacancies, gas and food prices are rising, and chaos reigns at the border – the President has shown his ineptitude to lead. He has instead chosen to rule by mandates. That is not the NH way – and today's small victory proved that." Majority Leader Osborne: “Welcome to New Hampshire, President Biden! I hope that we made you feel welcome by giving HB255 a bipartisan green light out of the Education Committee. An overwhelming majority of those who attended today’s hearing made it clear they do not support your vaccine mandate. This is the first step in pushing back against your vastly overreaching federal government and ensuring that Granite Staters who choose to exercise their right to health freedom will not lose their job.” “I applaud the members of th Education Committee who took this amendment up today and did the right thing for New Hampshire. While it is my hope that the president pays attention to the message we sent him on the heels of his visit today, I will not hold my breath. I look forward to passing this bill at our first opportunity when it comes before the House in January.” To view the House Calendar for the week, click the link below! | | "Calendar Link" | | | Senate Republican Update | | | | Become friends with NH Senate Republicans! Follow their page on ! | | Sen. Chuck Morse: Parents are being heard from Virginia to New Hampshire “I DON’T THINK parents should be telling schools what they should teach.” Those words from former Virginia Governor Terry McAuliffe are why he will not be the future Governor Terry McAuliffe. During a debate with Republican challenger Glenn Youngkin in late September, McAuliffe defended his veto of a bill that would have let parents prevent their children from being exposed to materials they found objectionable. The power of parents over their children’s education became the central issue of the gubernatorial campaign over the final month, with Youngkin stressing that parents should have ultimate control, while McAuliffe argued that parents should not be able to object to the decisions of public school officials. On election night, Virginia parents settled the question, delivering a shocking upset win to Youngkin in a state that Joe Biden had carried by 10 points just one year ago. The power of parents to guide the education their children receive proved to be decisive in the most watched race in the nation. Democrats attacked Youngkin for standing up for parents, falsely accusing him of wanting to ban books. They called him a racist for opposing the use of Critical Race Theory in public schools, arguing both that CRT didn’t really exist and that opposing it was a sign of White supremacy. Voters didn’t buy the smears. Parents have the power to bring about political change. Politicians ignore them at their peril. In New Hampshire, Republicans at the State House have been listening to parents and empowering them to be more involved in their children’s education. The COVID-19 pandemic put a huge strain on our students, and while some thrived, others struggled. We heard from parents that they needed more choices, and we listened. As part of the state budget, we created Education Freedom Accounts, which give parents the ability to direct how the state’s contribution to an adequate education is used. The program is limited to families earning 300% of the federal poverty level and structured similarly to New Hampshire’s successful Education Tax Credit Scholarship Program. Throughout the budget debate, we were told that expanding school choice would dismantle public education. Nonsense. Most parents will always choose their local district school, but there is no one-size-fits-all approach to schools. Every child learns differently, and EFAs are a way to provide a great education for kids that aren’t thriving in a traditional classroom environment. We provided new choices and parents responded. In the first year of this innovative program, 1,600 families in need have qualified for EFA scholarships. That’s 1,600 kids who will have a better path towards a great education. Parents are concerned that radical left-wing theories such as Critical Race Theory have seeped into our schools. Young kids are taught that they are born privileged and racist. This is unacceptable and should never have been allowed to happen in our schools. So we included anti-discrimination language in the recent state budget, ensuring that schools will not teach that one race is inherently superior or inferior to another. We need to teach our children our history, including those times when America has struggled to live up to our founding ideals. But we must never teach them racist lessons. Parents will be heard. If state legislators, school board members, or gubernatorial candidates ignore them, they will be heard at the voting booth. They will put themselves forward as candidates. They will give support to candidates who are willing to listen. They will rightly demand a greater say in what and how their children are taught. We listened to parents in New Hampshire this year. Glenn Youngkin listened to them in Virginia. I expect their voices to be heard all across the country in 2022. Sen. Chuck Morse (R-Salem) is president of the New Hampshire Senate. | | "RSVP HERE" | | | Did you know the RNC posts weekly news and articles? subject: Rittenhouse acquitted on all charges, House passes Build Back Better
body:
...and more. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a36a754b59a7/rittenhouse-acquitted-on-all-charges-house-passes-build-back-better?e=f6564bbe3b)

| | | |

| | | |

## The House passes Build Back Better...
CBO put out a report that said as written, Build Back Better would add $367 billion to the deficit over 10 years. The total spending is $1.7 trillion.I’m not great at economics, but the White House said this would cost $0. Isn’t $376 billion more than $0?Check out this spending breakdown and tune into _Spicer & Co_ tonight and we will be breaking it down.
| | | |
---
## Kyle Rittenhouse not guilty on all counts...
We’ve been following this case very closely on _Spicer & Co_ and Kyle Rittenhouse has been found NOT GUILTY on all charges. Tune in tonight, we will have a reporter live on the ground in Kenosha to bring us the latest.
| | | |
---
## Stories I’m reading...
[The Washington Post corrects, removes parts of two stories regarding the Steele Dossier](https://seanspicer.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d009170fb4604b5974b5521bd&id=ee393b908a&e=f6564bbe3b) — via Washington PostBig news incase you missed the “style” section where it was buried... executive editor said The Post could no longer stand by the accuracy of those elements of the story.
|

name: Herschel Walker
subject: Did you see this ratings flip?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC
body:
Our race in GA has been declared a “toss-up.” This is our best opportunity to pick up a seat in the Senate! |
---
**☞ GA Senate Ratings Change ☜
Lean Democratic -> Toss-Up**
Fellow Conservative,

BIG NEWS! The ratings changes are in.

Just weeks after we launched our campaign in Georgia for the U.S. Senate, political pundits are now rating the race as a "Toss-up," which is movement in our direction from the previous rating of "Leans Democratic."

If you've read anything about the outcome of the recent elections in Virginia and New Jersey, then you understand why Republicans are in a strong position to flip the Senate next year if we have the resources to communicate our message.

In fact, Gov.-elect Glenn Youngkin's success in Virginia (which had been trending Democratic in recent years) was so strong that it reverberated across the country and set off "Alarm Bells" with some of the more liberal election forecasters.

Take a look for yourself:

[](http://links.americansfordjt.com/u/click?_t=aff9c7dfb71242f8abc6b00341ebfdb2&_m=3d7143dedb534be282bfa3ad211f92ed&_e=6ulhb0bktpSY2kxWwbTEWtX8_TNsWh2TGlpNVQ3zfY4YL9U9IJdePaJclx0gFKC0LKr7VfxNUsBVdodoLIvA2PHQ4wWdut6YoLaBRAOK6n3B3gIzu_R-DxvUgP3vWJY1Pf-FKltsaRo6eyTCvp47pKoWBvMDxwFhsDn8_73TUoAMlLVru1bwQ4OpcHb3hc5xr-HqaZ75ojbQhnzdLWaNQkK3t6KfIeXxJEwAIn4smtnmEMFWdeQprOw-MnNNV1OGWjBgRX9SxFoilyBPw8Sgx7Ri8VaHeNwH3WHEBRolJ0F56Hj-RbHdDcIXmvsFvTDPHhivfqSuwNsTSAlMdlZwaTEZnrQgCqgv8EomHN4nlTyVA0iwJYAB0ZjhmgbpMjpNspSYzX0M4ePFtbkPUMpXbBoMD-iVymK5CBHIEeyewhhya1255jApwNCzRQyRbbUUZnSH8hthmDOehdIlBN-MFK3G0LvugFg1aXLP19F7P08%3D)
**I think after the recent election results in Virginia, every election prognosticator in the country realizes that our Senate race in Georgia is a toss-up.**

While I'm not a career politician, it's clear as day that national Democrats are going to see this ratings change as a signal they need to pour as much money as humanly possible into this race to keep this Senate seat in their column.

Therefore, we _must_ increase our own fundraising efforts to beat them. If you can make an investment in this critical toss-up race, please know there is no better time than today. **[This is a race in which every SINGLE DOLLAR is going to count. clean_body: Our race in GA has been declared a “toss-up.” This is our best opportunity to pick up a seat in the Senate! | ☞ GA Senate Ratings Change ☜ Lean Democratic -> Toss-Up Fellow Conservative, BIG NEWS! The ratings changes are in. Just weeks after we launched our campaign in Georgia for the U.S. Senate, political pundits are now rating the race as a "Toss-up," which is movement in our direction from the previous rating of "Leans Democratic." If you've read anything about the outcome of the recent elections in Virginia and New Jersey, then you understand why Republicans are in a strong position to flip the Senate next year if we have the resources to communicate our message. In fact, Gov.-elect Glenn Youngkin's success in Virginia (which had been trending Democratic in recent years) was so strong that it reverberated across the country and set off "Alarm Bells" with some of the more liberal election forecasters. Take a look for yourself: I think after the recent election results in Virginia, every election prognosticator in the country realizes that our Senate race in Georgia is a toss-up. While I'm not a career politician, it's clear as day that national Democrats are going to see this ratings change as a signal they need to pour as much money as humanly possible into this race to keep this Senate seat in their column. Therefore, we must increase our own fundraising efforts to beat them. If you can make an investment in this critical toss-up race, please know there is no better time than today. My opponent was THE TOP FUNDRAISER IN THE ENTIRE COUNTRY over the last three months. We've got to be able to beat them at their own game. While the ratings change is certainly encouraging, it's also going to motivate the Democrats to double down. Please stand with us at this important moment. Thank you, Herschel Walker | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

Peter,

**Have you heard the news?** _**The Jury just announced their verdict in the Kyle Rittenhouse case.**_

This is a groundbreaking case because Kyle Rittenhouse was defending himself and the community of Kenosha from rioters and looters. Some called him a right-wing radical, while others called him a law-abiding patriot.

The talking heads on TV are already saying how this verdict will have a lasting impact on our nation, but maybe the real people of America, like you Peter, don’t really care. [**Either way, if you got 30 seconds, can you let me know if you think the Jury got this verdict right or not?**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=11c12f949e684f19a22318c0c3037b68&_e=Cg2GhZLe8RFD-OJduDSWCwhU1MK1t0yVAQZGb2iEdGcxtK-t-_lgAc6MJQFd1JnpzbF32OtTtAwXfuWyS35BGKvixb9lV7w9OkOdS21ciMPVx15PsIFP_h267lbh_gLdnBPGrUBnhuKtbfelJvXqoruBT5rQGr8hkF7ZZFK7leIG1vVK18IT2mlankbeRVrlcii_1zdqUtWtu1HMBSY6BNhktAq1AgBeI76x4waJL7l1vOGmqTZP4vtTUhzwyxX2sC3EHKNgu6IWbg_ZxiGbXhTCavKxIKDSX7rMxKLi5ncLAyTrxZL0VNRAECKhp-fAHoEQ4SPoQX025VwD2fK9YX1_GEuoIRmmWyjm98qA9QQl9VZRvnJsD6sgLfCLV5PYn3r3ZKekSKYNXpNf_8aFxPPYgHv5Jwgj3_JL_hioJ79ht0WNPqey2YSorh1_c73mWbf4VbX4jfeMkhPNhNEsElDWKemq-gn9sU1kZ_z0wBrcbr-Gq8tewXVjqJQN-EyaITUo1bFHXllccee3uCoI90w7Sj3jVn0oi4OigLvB2wZlRRYotKaE2V8fXn_hcwTg)

[** DID THE KYLE RITTENHOUSE JURY MAKE THE RIGHT VERDICT? **](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=11c12f949e684f19a22318c0c3037b68&_e=Cg2GhZLe8RFD-OJduDSWCwhU1MK1t0yVAQZGb2iEdGcxtK-t-_lgAc6MJQFd1JnpzbF32OtTtAwXfuWyS35BGKvixb9lV7w9OkOdS21ciMPVx15PsIFP_h267lbh_gLdnBPGrUBnhuKtbfelJvXqoruBT5rQGr8hkF7ZZFK7leIG1vVK18IT2mlankbeRVrlcii_1zdqUtWtu1HMBSY6BNhktAq1AgBeI76x4waJL7l1vOGmqTZP4vtTUhzwyxX2sC3EHKNgu6IWbg_ZxiGbXhTCavKxIKDSX7rMxKLi5ncLAyTrxZL0VNRAECKhp-fAHoEQ4SPoQX025VwD2fK9YX1_GEuoIRmmWyjm98qA9QR8DOUZ1QjwhfLXgCeEE0nvI6zezh83HS7IY5rZBp7HwT9uQPgWanurOUrOSTlIkeuoe_-ZyV7eiC3rgwS-8S1xzxXHh2kEMLERmw875yQ_uy6q-K9gmD9DGEhVr34ommHtCbWtek7u8nepUFYCcNMAj7f6EIGjm_Q_KWUazNMm-B8Vftv7EM8eEjLYTFZpoN2kBJKtUsooxm9FqDRaOYQg)
I’m sure this is going to be appealed to a higher court and brought up again at some point. Still, I think getting your feedback on this case is important, and [**I want to see if you think the jury was fair and made the right decision, or if you think they got the verdict wrong?**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=11c12f949e684f19a22318c0c3037b68&_e=Cg2GhZLe8RFD-OJduDSWCwhU1MK1t0yVAQZGb2iEdGcxtK-t-_lgAc6MJQFd1JnpzbF32OtTtAwXfuWyS35BGKvixb9lV7w9OkOdS21ciMPVx15PsIFP_h267lbh_gLdnBPGrUBnhuKtbfelJvXqoruBT5rQGr8hkF7ZZFK7leIG1vVK18IT2mlankbeRVrlcii_1zdqUtWtu1HMBSY6BNhktAq1AgBeI76x4waJL7l1vOGmqTZP4vtTUhzwyxX2sC3EHKNgu6IWbg_ZxiGbXhTCavKxIKDSX7rMxKLi5ncLAyTrxZL0VNRAECKhp-fAHoEQ4SPoQX025VwD2fK9YX1_GEuoIRmmWyjm98qA9QRb7M-LycUiSFyfLQylgU02v5T6OED_iFOF_-96hKp6HdSoPPeVUxnb2S7UavA6AOQF2FoHCKFpmxImAXOzfMgzgNexXXRT6JxbfSiiHuYwE3-MJMeY2MGqFx0fdX376LvTU-hqUUblRynukcHJGU2oaWjAB22mBEEjTIUeaCeAswKQYJ2FhlS2rn03rG3IYhURkt9u0pkWkMbjeIFp9I8w)

name: Kina Collins
subject: Today, I grieve for our country, again.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KINA COLLINS FOR CONGRESS** ](https://u1584542
body:
Kyle Rittenhouse, who killed two protesters last year at a BLM rally in Wisconsin and injured a third, was acquitted of all charges. | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yOQozAy4249GfohatM8XLCP7Gyf6bzrkjN8sXh_JjXvDm21fvmGLhqUe-quR3_x0bPZYqqRmKw-ebpnqlyWdsbb4WUxyyDpjjPMxiSxHH8sx8gcSBWf3euzmaHu2A2DbufzHwPTwHNAEtjBBesDnfrGK-UCc6HR1DrEaSTrAY_j7922tJW71m_irBepyd8p5jlgL8aJiygc3ztZlZkin9eDZuZjqq5Am4SUaMZGB-mxeppGTwKt3F4tkovOz6T8Nl34-N-xLD2-dplNK254hUOh82HI7swPy1ST4K1J3isPSdurqCLtsiLGvDZQvBHHUrOHHKhIH4ozjCSWZjP6qTSsdJs1eNHOvJi-um531QYjdubOqusGYdlU6uh6lK0Rvg/3h5/uXgRcyFkQMmAc9HXE-ShoA/h0/FMrGHXt2267_zX9mk4wRmJKKvfTR-IHxVXWJWcJkHcc) Team, As I’m sure you’re aware of at this point, today, Kyle Rittenhouse, who killed two protesters last year at a BLM rally in Wisconsin and injured a third, was acquitted of all charges. As we as a community and country process another moment of injustice that has become all too familiar, I wanted to share my thoughts with you. This outcome is the result of a legal system built to uphold and maintain white supremacy. There are Black kids sitting in jail right now for a bag of weed. Yet Kyle Rittenhouse illegally got a gun, went to a protest across state lines, and killed two people and injured another in an act of white supremacist vigilante violence — and won’t spend a day in prison. That Rittenhouse walks free while so many Black and brown kids will spend decades in cages for nonviolent crimes is a damning indictment of a legal system incapable of carrying out true justice. I’m grieving for the families of the victims, who have been denied accountability and justice. I’m standing in solidarity with the organizers and activists working tirelessly to change a system that has devastated our communities for too long. It’s all of our responsibility to carry on the fight for change and for justice. Sending love to all those still processing this tragedy and hoping that our country’s future can include a vision of justice rooted in equality and accountability instead of racism and inequity.
In solidarity, Kina Collins

| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

name: Cicely Davis
subject: Rittenhouse
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CICELY DAVIS FOR CONGRESS
body:
Fellow Conservative, Do you think the jury made the right call in the Kyle Rittenhouse case? |
---
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be08ab7ba4054f70ba711d601c026053&_e=ngRjohCHvHVkbJStVeH4Qe0G1T1vdcTYD64OIWYuu235We57cXK2SteL6ZjkM_-6Zgv4DGoC9FyJE3E2795yZ32N1q4WIfjBg23NKxIe0cio3t5CtnTe3GLhqTk8F-ERkjs7ukkifHOFb2DlVz1VTRyVuKppkfS48xudQid1WRbdnQCI8gexlaTu8xpj0QQH0QSUP1oSBwvaSOZlpaDFU1EspKDR385qLk1SLXHi1Xjq9f1n9cPRI6jB-NApKkKPF1F9pEm4uQvTTnhr1IeJhPoh6e4cVKiqQDfYNCs62hZPXjLqHM0UCIYdZDS3RDUC-8IDmlayLvKar5_glQffV9PtruTtHJ6nQok_4O48Zi4djqmk5mxYDvVKx4A1OWKmW-OJiaQhTvEBFl0h5Vkp4f0ibA48nsXn4h_fwXBXrFO3_kiZCiZMtIfMF63arFsSioiFkQlwsbUJF0R2SDzLIwh2QKEcUT9Je0dMbr29bXdFoss7oBzkGq_XIdWieUef6DLPIYQ5P02xaQ61NN4Yb2w9ewygJ8u3ewXZi_wRvfPWdx14FeS6Cq-jqUnl11--)
Fellow Conservative,

**Have you heard the news?** _**The Jury just announced their verdict in the Kyle Rittenhouse case.**_

This is a groundbreaking case because Kyle Rittenhouse was defending himself and the community of Kenosha from rioters and looters. Some called him a right-wing radical, while others called him a law-abiding patriot.

The talking heads on TV are already saying how this verdict will have a lasting impact on our nation, but maybe the real people of America, like you Fellow Conservative, don’t really care. [**Either way, if you got 30 seconds, can you let me know if you think the Jury got this verdict right or not?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be08ab7ba4054f70ba711d601c026053&_e=ngRjohCHvHVkbJStVeH4Qe0G1T1vdcTYD64OIWYuu235We57cXK2SteL6ZjkM_-6Zgv4DGoC9FyJE3E2795yZ32N1q4WIfjBg23NKxIe0cio3t5CtnTe3GLhqTk8F-ERkjs7ukkifHOFb2DlVz1VTRyVuKppkfS48xudQid1WRbdnQCI8gexlaTu8xpj0QQH0QSUP1oSBwvaSOZlpaDFU1EspKDR385qLk1SLXHi1Xjq9f1n9cPRI6jB-NApKkKPF1F9pEm4uQvTTnhr1IeJhPoh6e4cVKiqQDfYNCs62hZPXjLqHM0UCIYdZDS3RDUC-8IDmlayLvKar5_glQffV9PtruTtHJ6nQok_4O48Zi50c1Ho86p5Tp0JaiJ-Fg91bg-TkZu0i-g-Uczkst8kECoMsNVdpxmllchemj4SPjvheFuur_xb-J27WsdkHfht-IwvBuCXPZDEV5dvkUYoFE9D2zIBG8Zx-zYleQgG4y413QNDFU_c8iurKQGdm9FpWHnjm0KKHRnDTC9NM75E7rHUm-tPLl5khCbhtecDX6i37VnIsQfVYIGe2ZkdR9_J)

[** DID THE KYLE RITTENHOUSE JURY MAKE THE RIGHT VERDICT? **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be08ab7ba4054f70ba711d601c026053&_e=ngRjohCHvHVkbJStVeH4Qe0G1T1vdcTYD64OIWYuu235We57cXK2SteL6ZjkM_-6Zgv4DGoC9FyJE3E2795yZ32N1q4WIfjBg23NKxIe0cio3t5CtnTe3GLhqTk8F-ERkjs7ukkifHOFb2DlVz1VTRyVuKppkfS48xudQid1WRbdnQCI8gexlaTu8xpj0QQH0QSUP1oSBwvaSOZlpaDFU1EspKDR385qLk1SLXHi1Xjq9f1n9cPRI6jB-NApKkKPF1F9pEm4uQvTTnhr1IeJhPoh6e4cVKiqQDfYNCs62hZPXjLqHM0UCIYdZDS3RDUC-8IDmlayLvKar5_glQffV9PtruTtHJ6nQok_4O48Zi7STfqTQqZfxdasNH0Y1EA8v0yfFVDKEvN9ylV4FSs_61B6EWhBgF3o8_-VSdQGxnx54BWb8_iXP2ljCUD7RttLItbuZj9E7nEH1V5VIOIKh5pDZQB2q6CSYq04_ikuQpIZCokXVxO_pwKqZV3Sihr9RQK2wp0GotzZ3k10Q1AXlnLvdC9m7ZvssKDSxsnXoH34oe1InK2qHIWN8IDFfjGz)
I’m sure this is going to be appealed to a higher court and brought up again at some point. Still, I think getting your feedback on this case is important, and [**I want to see if you think the jury was fair and made the right decision, or if you think they got the verdict wrong?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=be08ab7ba4054f70ba711d601c026053&_e=ngRjohCHvHVkbJStVeH4Qe0G1T1vdcTYD64OIWYuu235We57cXK2SteL6ZjkM_-6Zgv4DGoC9FyJE3E2795yZ32N1q4WIfjBg23NKxIe0cio3t5CtnTe3GLhqTk8F-ERkjs7ukkifHOFb2DlVz1VTRyVuKppkfS48xudQid1WRbdnQCI8gexlaTu8xpj0QQH0QSUP1oSBwvaSOZlpaDFU1EspKDR385qLk1SLXHi1Xjq9f1n9cPRI6jB-NApKkKPF1F9pEm4uQvTTnhr1IeJhPoh6e4cVKiqQDfYNCs62hZPXjLqHM0UCIYdZDS3RDUC-8IDmlayLvKar5_glQffV9PtruTtHJ6nQok_4O48Zi4ec1wGnC9E-Qa8wfaMQ_wg73Cl-xvEoHZ6xRjswB7ncVptlU2pQuIEbJLZY1QPKLJeT6FQC_HY5mW_dbfFsvmHj7rLWiBuxtgH5wE0xohM-zVJO0CGyAI_hBwQmjW39pBfgjpr4TIUaiTlzJ5TaO4ydponFZ1lyaMepO4oq4efv6B2HMPNW0-by4KEzS1jgXdCoUxRgEbe4Gm1JFEjL5Hp)

clean_body: Fellow Conservative, Do you think the jury made the right call in the Kyle Rittenhouse case? | -- Fellow Conservative, Have you heard the news? The Jury just announced their verdict in the Kyle Rittenhouse case. This is a groundbreaking case because Kyle Rittenhouse was defending himself and the community of Kenosha from rioters and looters. Some called him a right-wing radical, while others called him a law-abiding patriot. The talking heads on TV are already saying how this verdict will have a lasting impact on our nation, but maybe the real people of America, like you Fellow Conservative, don’t really care. I’m sure this is going to be appealed to a higher court and brought up again at some point. Still, I think getting your feedback on this case is important, and Thank you for your time, I’m looking forward to your response, Fellow Conservative. Cicely Davis | | PAID FOR BY CICELY DAVIS FOR CONGRESS FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

Peter,

I want to take a moment to say congratulations to Kyle Rittenhouse on his full acquittal. He should never have been charged. Self-defense is not a crime.

Having seen the mayhem caused by rioters and looters in Portland and Seattle last year, **this judgement, in my opinion, sends a message to all Americans that our judicial system is working the way that it is supposed to work**.

Kyle Rittenhouse should have never gone to Kenosha, Wisconsin in the first place; the police officers of Kenosha and the national guardsmen of Wisconsin should have been able to quell the riots that broke out last summer, but the inept Democrat governor of Wisconsin --- along with governors in blue states all over the country last year -- opted not to hold looters and rioters accountable out of fear of looking racist.

**Do you think the jury got this one right? I’m curious to know your opinion.**

name: Team Inslee
subject: A historic investment
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
So we can build a clean energy economy that works for all of us.

| [](https://links.e.jayinslee.com/els/v2/x4r9S2PvpmcG/VXJFYnh3SC9VZHVudTZoanVmNktGanlTZFpmVm5GejhGSFNTSlF0cXhBU0NNdWFHRy9QZFdKak1FUlZKK1BuMzNRK0tGQ2dJRkY3MmdYZlk0QnNUa1ovRCtvOXY1WGtWK1pxZ2VOOVQ1MFU9S0/TmRqZit4Y0ZEZnFFSmhVT2JMZTBrRWpISDVLSGRadjFZQTFhbnoxVjF4ST0S1) Friend -- The Build Back Better Act will make historic investments to combat the climate crisis, while helping build a just economy that works for all Americans. **Today, I am thrilled that the House made history and passed this momentous bill. But now the Senate must act and they have been dragging their feet. Why? Because Big Oil is doing all they can to kneecap this bill and our response to the climate crisis.** The fate of our planet and our species is on the line. The Build Back Better Act is an investment into our shared future. We can't afford to fail. But to get this bill through the Senate and onto President Biden's desk, we'll need to make sure our demand for action rings out loudly and clearly. [_**Please, add your name right now to our petition demanding the Senate move to immediately pass the Build Back Better Act.**_](https://links.e.jayinslee.com/els/v2/8EW3h~xRZbtY/VXJFYnh3SC9VZHVudTZoanVmNktGanlTZFpmVm5GejhGSFNTSlF0cXhBU0NNdWFHRy9QZFdKak1FUlZKK1BuMzNRK0tGQ2dJRkY3MmdYZlk0QnNUa1ovRCtvOXY1WGtWK1pxZ2VOOVQ1MFU9S0/TmRqZit4Y0ZEZnFFSmhVT2JMZTBrRWpISDVLSGRadjFZQTFhbnoxVjF4ST0S1) Let's build a clean energy economy that works for all of us. Thanks for taking action, Team Inslee | [ADD NAME](https://links.e.jayinslee.com/els/v2/9bq3h02pjgF9/VXJFYnh3SC9VZHVudTZoanVmNktGanlTZFpmVm5GejhGSFNTSlF0cXhBU0NNdWFHRy9QZFdKak1FUlZKK1BuMzNRK0tGQ2dJRkY3MmdYZlk0QnNUa1ovRCtvOXY1WGtWK1pxZ2VOOVQ1MFU9S0/TmRqZit4Y0ZEZnFFSmhVT2JMZTBrRWpISDVLSGRadjFZQTFhbnoxVjF4ST0S1)

Because Big Oil is doing all they can to kneecap this bill and our response to the climate crisis. The fate of our planet and our species is on the line. clean_body: I was disappointed to see you never responded. If you would prefer not to receive future emails, visit here. I'm hoping this was a mistake. My name is Jordan from House Republican Headquarters. The Chairman of the Republican National Committee reached out to a select group of Conservatives... And I was disappointed to see you never responded. We’re showing her a list TOMORROW of Conservatives who answered her pressing call to action -- and I repeat: your name is missing. \---------- Forwarded message --------- To: From: RNC Chairman Ronna McDaniel Subject: This is your formal notice from Republican HQ: Official Message From The Chairman Of The Republican National Committee FORMAL NOTICE FROM REPUBLICAN HQ: A FEW MORE SPOTS JUST OPENED TO BECOME A TOP TRUMP PATRIOT Hello Friend, President Donald J. Trump just sent out a clear directive to Conservatives everywhere: we must win back the House for the American People. I'm taking his call to action with the utmost seriousness. I certainly hope you do too. I managed to reserve a spot specifically for you. You have from RIGHT NOW until midnight to claim it. After that, we 'll be forced to move on!** This is your formal notice: I reserved your spot under the following email: \-- please claim it before tonight. Sincerely, Ronna McDaniel Chairman of The Republican National Committee Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC. 320 First Street SE, Washington, DC 20003 Contributions to TEAM SCALISE, or any participating committee, are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, national banks, labor unions, foreign nationals without "green cards," and federal government contractors are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and employer of each individual who contributes over $200 in a calendar year. 320 First Street SE Washington, DC 20003 Don't want to receive any more of our emails? .

subject: Please.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
→ | ****
---
| _PLEASE, Friend.

**Have you abandoned Trump? We hope this isn’t true.**

Kevin McCarthy has lost it, folks.

In a bizarre attempt to stop the House from passing the Build Back Better Act, Kevin McCarthy gave a rambling mess of a speech for over 8 hours on the House floor. He went from topic to topic trying to delay the passage of a bill that would bring millions in funding to his own district.

His temper tantrum lasted until 1 A.M., and even members of his own party seemed tired of his directionless rambling.

**[If this is what Kevin McCarthy thinks leadership looks like, he’s sorely mistaken. Donate to Bruno Amato’s campaign to unseat him right now!](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xk9NwEyjAoWg-sb9VIPpLoJUmEIvv2tpr47ZCn3v2NNpsLUh79KTmh02WqO7iw5RGldPvAj8peRoo2IdD0dc8b9dKPkvr-4hGszloTCXBqlS6qKrKHzKB4urYSE3XridFBUOQwo9OVJZazNtKzP3e7vk5ppw4AovoPdUZ9d-6qf016zXoWMwYTfHJZew7Q6FZ9fErJd7q8mcfswSrYlqxb4LotH1KADbvdSygNtVbTuJoW8nmWqXzLtnvUndsLbBuFdsKh39xi9dhhUo8EvLKx0Y6WD3HIFP5u5MOof45AtCd2TFh9HEzM5uvm5d_JUJHkNxYmaohS0JvF2dYShpRUX_oPltl8Msg8ZwnP1zit6A/3h5/eOyJSrayQpChnWLUwELkvQ/h1/M4g1-VT8nxOBuyqR2SOs6IPaieZsad2AMxlmibDtSCo)**

You’d think there was a point to McCarthy’s rant, but there wasn’t. The Build Back Better Act was still passed, and Americans are going to be better off because of it. Our economy will flourish and we’ll have the funding we need to fix America’s deep inequities and take on the challenges of the future like climate change.

**No thanks to Kevin.

[McCarthy is no use to the American people. Chip today to our campaign to unseat him and elect a leader who won’t stand in the way of progress.
